PROJECT_CONTEXT.md.purpose: High-level repo map and durable project context.
read_when: Starting broad repo work
Explaining app architecture
Verifying setup or product context
skip_when: A narrow file-level pass already has scope and registry context

README.md.purpose: Human setup and runbook entry point.
read_when: Running the app
Checking environment commands
Helping the user self-serve
skip_when: Only source refactoring is needed

docs/widget_cleanup_todo.md.purpose: Short human-readable widget cleanup status pointer, including stream lifecycle and branded error UI queue summaries.
read_when: User asks what is next in widget cleanup
Need current pending queue in prose form
Need a quick summary of stream lifecycle or branded error UI debt
skip_when: Use backlog.json for machine-readable current state
Use archived history only for old detailed findings

docs/widget_catalog.md.purpose: Widget inventory and feature ownership map.
read_when: Auditing a specific feature's widget surface
Checking whether a primitive already exists
Updating widget APIs, screen ownership models, sliver/tab structures, or reusable design-system roles
skip_when: Only process rules are needed; use rules.json instead

docs/ui_layout_spacing.md.purpose: Durable UI spacing and layout contracts for screen gutters, tab body insets, sliver body gaps, and card/photo spacing.
read_when: Changing shared screen padding or tab body insets
Adding layout constants intended to be reused across sibling widgets
Debugging inconsistent spacing between sliver/tab bodies
skip_when: The task is pure business logic or repository/controller work
A local component uses a one-off spacing value that is not becoming a reusable contract

docs/audit_registry/rules.json.purpose: Active/watch/archived recursive audit rules, including provider stream lifecycle constraints and branded error UI requirements.
read_when: Starting an audit or cleanup pass
Adding or updating recurring process rules
Checking whether widget catalog updates are required
Changing realtime Firestore stream providers or listener lifecycles
Changing app-facing error presentation
skip_when: A task is a trivial one-off command

docs/controller_patterns.md.purpose: Current controller, view-model, and realtime stream lifecycle boundary rules.
read_when: Controller/state-management/refactor work
Moving business logic out of widgets
Changing keepAlive, autoDispose, or retained-tab stream ownership
skip_when: Pure visual primitive work

docs/sliver_layout_guide.md.purpose: Sliver-native layout guide and Catch-specific scroll contracts, including the scroll-away title plus pinned bottom-row header rule.
read_when: Changing CustomScrollView, SliverList, SliverPersistentHeader, or NestedScrollView surfaces
Debugging blank/overflow scroll bugs
skip_when: No scroll/sliver behavior is involved; use SLIVER-001 summary instead

read_when: Need historical provider inventory or architecture rationale
skip_when: Current controller rules are enough; prefer controller_patterns.md

docs/error-handling-audit.md.purpose: Error handling architecture snapshot, Error 101 guide, app error catalogue, remediation map, and branded error surface guidance.
read_when: Changing exceptions, stable error codes, error mapping, logging, or user-facing error rendering
Deciding whether a repeated failure deserves a new AppException subclass
Replacing raw AsyncValue error branches
skip_when: No error behavior is touched

read_when: Prioritizing launch blockers
Revalidating product/security/ops risks
skip_when: Assuming current status without re-verification

docs/firestore_functions_data_contract_t.purpose: Firestore rules, Cloud Functions boundaries, schema drift, data-contract tracker, and Firestore rules emulator-test workflow.
read_when: Changing Firestore rules
Changing Functions mutation boundaries
Changing shared data contracts
Running or diagnosing Firestore rules tests
skip_when: Pure Flutter presentation work

docs/backend_operation_catalog.md.purpose: Human-readable backend write ownership catalog covering direct client writes, callable-owned mutations, trigger-owned projections, server-only collections, and notification starting points.
read_when: Changing a repository write or callable interface
Changing Firestore rules for a write path
Adding Cloud Functions or trigger-owned projections
Planning notifications or activity timeline fan-out
skip_when: No backend write ownership or notification behavior is touched

docs/firestore_relationship_documents_mi.purpose: Persistent tracker for retired relationship arrays, edge/action documents, match-scoped messages, beta reset strategy, Firestore rules simplification after relationship migration, and relationship-doc deletion/anonymization cleanup.
read_when: Changing run-club membership, run participation, saved-run, match-message, or relationship-array behavior
Planning Firestore rules simplification after relationship-doc migration
Planning beta data reset or edge-doc validation before deploy
Writing or running relationship validation or legacy chat migration scripts
Planning account, run, club, review, match, or chat deletion/anonymization cleanup
skip_when: No Firestore relationship, migration, or deletion contract is touched

read_when: Changing domain models used by Functions
Regenerating Firestore TypeScript interfaces
skip_when: No schema/type contract changes

docs/deploy_runbook_2026_05_04.md.purpose: May 2026 relationship-doc deployment sequence, prerequisites, smoke tests, and Remote Config force-update timing.
read_when: Deploying Firebase/Functions/rules/storage changes
Rolling out relationship-document schema changes
Planning Remote Config minimum-build gates
skip_when: Not deploying

read_when: User asks about quality rating or compensation-bar assessment
skip_when: Current verification has not been refreshed

docs/audit_registry/README.md.purpose: Recursive audit workflow, registry files, pruning policy, and completion criteria.
read_when: Starting any repeated cleanup/audit pass
skip_when: Task is a trivial one-off command

docs/audit_registry/backlog.json.purpose: Active backlog, next-up queue, stable debt ids, and scanner counts.
read_when: Choosing the next audit task
Updating debt status or scanner snapshots
Answering what remains pending
skip_when: A user only needs high-level prose from widget_cleanup_todo.md

docs/audit_registry/doc_versions.json.purpose: Version metadata and read policies for durable docs.
read_when: Adding, deleting, renaming, archiving, or materially changing durable docs
Checking whether markdown front matter and registry metadata agree
skip_when: No durable docs or read policies changed

docs/audit_registry/doc_summaries.json.purpose: Compact read/skip policies for durable docs.
read_when: Deciding which docs to open for a task
Changing doc read policies or durable doc ownership
skip_when: The relevant doc was already identified by a stable debt id
